title: MQTT Protocol for IoT Sensor Data
content: MQTT (Message Queuing Telemetry Transport) is a lightweight, publish–subscribe messaging protocol designed for constrained IoT devices over low-bandwidth, high-latency networks.
extended_content: MQTT v3.1.1 / v5.0 uses a central broker (e.g., Mosquitto, AWS IoT Core).  Clients publish to topics (e.g., 'station/01/temperature'); subscribers receive matching messages.  QoS levels: 0 (at most once), 1 (at least once), 2 (exactly once).  Minimum header overhead is 2 bytes.  TLS encryption adds ≈ 5–10 kB of code on constrained MCUs. For weather stations, MQTT over LoRaWAN gateways or WiFi is common; last-will messages notify subscribers of connection loss.
source: MQTT v3.1.1 OASIS Standard (2014); MQTT v5.0 OASIS Standard (2019)
